content.problemStatement:
Chef got interested in bits and wanted to learn about them, so his friend Pintu gave him a special function F(X,Y,Z)=(X∧Z)⋅(Y∧Z)F(X,Y,Z) = (X \wedge Z) \cdot (Y \wedge Z)F(X,Y,Z)=(X∧Z)⋅(Y∧Z), where ∧\wedge∧ is the bitwise AND operator and X,Y,ZX, Y, ZX,Y,Z are non-negative integers.

Pintu wants Chef to maximise the function F(X,Y,Z)F(X,Y,Z)F(X,Y,Z) for given XXX and YYY by choosing an appropriate ZZZ. However, to make it interesting, Pintu also gave Chef limits LLL and RRR for ZZZ. In other words, he wants Chef to find a non-negative integer ZZZ (L≤Z≤RL \le Z \le RL≤Z≤R) such that F(X,Y,Z)=maxL≤k≤R(F(X,Y,k))F(X,Y,Z) = \mathrm{max}_{L \le k \le R} \left( F(X,Y,k) \right)F(X,Y,Z)=maxL≤k≤R​(F(X,Y,k)). If there is more than one such value of ZZZ, he should find the smallest one in the range [L,R][L, R][L,R].

Since Chef is busy cooking in the kitchen, he needs you to help him solve this problem.

Note: XXX, YYY, LLL and RRR are chosen in such a way that maxL≤k≤R(F(X,Y,k))\mathrm{max}_{L \le k \le R} \left( F(X,Y,k) \right)maxL≤k≤R​(F(X,Y,k)) never exceeds 2622^{62}262.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains four space-separated integers XXX, YYY, LLL and RRR.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the smallest value of ZZZ in the given range that maximises FFF.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Here, Z=15Z = 15Z=15 maximises the function, since F(7,12,15)=84F(7,12,15) = 84F(7,12,15)=84. It is impossible to reach F>84F \gt 84F>84 with any ZZZ in the given range.
Example case 2: The smallest ZZZ which maximises FFF is Z=7Z = 7Z=7, and the value of FFF for this ZZZ is 282828.
